files.name: QueryEngine.ts
files.summary: This file implements the core query execution engine for Claude conversations. QueryEngine owns a conversation's lifecycle—managing messages, state, and API interactions across multiple turns—while the `ask()` function provides a simpler one-shot wrapper around it. The engine processes user input, constructs system prompts, invokes the Claude API via a lower-level `query()` function, handles tool use and permissions, tracks costs and usage, manages session persistence, and yields structured SDK messages (system init, assistant responses, results, errors) to callers. It also supports advanced features like thinking modes, message compaction (snipping), structured output validation, budget limits, and file history snapshots.

files.name: README.md
files.summary: This README documents a security incident where Claude Code's complete source code was accidentally exposed via sourcemap files published to npm. It serves as both a detailed technical breakdown of what was leaked and an analysis of the tool's internal architecture, revealing numerous unreleased features like a Tamagotchi-style companion pet system ("Buddy"), an always-on proactive assistant mode ("KAIROS"), multi-agent orchestration capabilities, and a background memory consolidation engine called "Dream." The document explains how the leak occurred (sourcemaps containing unminified source code weren't excluded from the npm package), catalogs 40+ internal tools and feature flags, and discusses Anthropic's internal security practices including an "Undercover Mode" that prevents employees from accidentally revealing internal information in public commits.

files.name: Task.ts
files.summary: This file defines the core type system and utilities for managing tasks across the system. It establishes what tasks are (local bash commands, agents, workflows, etc.), their lifecycle states (pending through terminal states), and provides helpers to generate unique task IDs with type-specific prefixes and create base task state objects. The terminal state detection function helps prevent operations on completed or failed tasks, while the Task interface defines a minimal contract that all task implementations must satisfy for cleanup and termination.

files.name: Tool.ts
files.summary: This file defines the core type system and architecture for tools in an AI agent framework. It establishes the `Tool` interface—a comprehensive contract that every tool must implement—covering execution (`call`), schema definitions (`inputSchema`, `outputSchema`), permissions, UI rendering, progress tracking, and metadata. The file also exports helper types like `ToolUseContext` (which bundles execution environment, state management, and callbacks), `ToolPermissionContext` (for permission modes and rules), and utility functions like `buildTool` that apply safe defaults to tool definitions. This is foundational infrastructure that enables the framework to uniformly manage diverse tools (file operations, bash, MCP servers, etc.) with consistent permission checking, progress reporting, and rendering behavior.

files.name: assistant/sessionHistory.ts
files.summary: This module provides paginated access to session event history from a backend API. It sets up OAuth authentication once and reuses it across multiple page requests, allowing callers to fetch the latest events or load older events by cursor to support infinite-scroll or backward-pagination patterns. The module abstracts away API details like headers, URLs, and response parsing behind a simple interface.

files.name: bridge/bridgeApi.ts
files.summary: This file implements the HTTP client for communicating with the Bridge API, which manages remote control environments and work sessions. It provides methods to register environments, poll for incoming work tasks, acknowledge and stop work, manage sessions, send heartbeats, and handle permission events, all while managing OAuth authentication with retry logic and trusted device validation. The module also validates server-provided IDs to prevent injection attacks and defines fatal vs. retriable error handling specific to Bridge API responses.

files.name: bridge/bridgeConfig.ts
files.summary: This file centralizes bridge authentication and URL configuration, eliminating duplicated dev overrides that were scattered across multiple parts of the codebase. It provides getters that check for ant-only environment variable overrides first (for local development), then fall back to the actual OAuth store and production config. Callers can use either the override-aware versions or the override getters directly depending on their auth source.

files.name: bridge/bridgeDebug.ts
files.summary: This file provides fault-injection testing capabilities for the bridge API client, allowing developers to manually trigger specific failure scenarios (like poll 404 errors, WebSocket closures, and transient network failures) to validate recovery code paths. It exposes a debug handle with methods to inject faults, force reconnections, and monitor bridge state, while wrapping the real API client to intercept calls and throw injected errors—this is only active in internal "ant" builds and has zero overhead in production.

files.name: bridge/bridgeEnabled.ts
files.summary: This file provides runtime entitlement and feature-gating checks for Remote Control (bridge mode), which allows Claude to control a user's development environment. It determines whether users can access bridge functionality based on subscription status (claude.ai OAuth required), GrowthBook feature flags, and CLI version requirements, while also offering diagnostic messages to explain why the feature might be unavailable. The functions here act as centralized guards that prevent unauthorized access and handle the transition between different bridge implementations (v1 env-based vs v2 env-less).

files.name: bridge/bridgeMessaging.ts
files.summary: This module provides shared, pure utility functions for handling bridge messages between the SDK and REPL core. It contains type guards for discriminating SDK message types, message routing logic that deduplicates echoes and re-deliveries, handlers for server-initiated control requests (like interrupt or set_model), and a bounded ring-buffer set for efficiently tracking recent message UUIDs. The code is deliberately stateless and parameterized so both environment-aware and environment-less bridge implementations can reuse the same message-handling machinery without coupling to specific bridge state.

files.name: bridge/bridgePermissionCallbacks.ts
files.summary: Defines the contract for permission-based communication between a backend service and a web bridge, specifying callback signatures for sending/receiving permission requests and responses. Includes a type guard to safely validate permission response payloads without unsafe type casting.

files.name: bridge/bridgePointer.ts
files.summary: This file manages crash-recovery pointers for Remote Control sessions, enabling the `claude remote-control --continue` command to resume interrupted bridge sessions. It persists session metadata (ID, environment ID, source) to disk with a 4-hour TTL based on file modification time, automatically deleting stale pointers to prevent orphaned state. For multi-worktree Git repos, it intelligently searches across worktree siblings to find the freshest pointer when resuming, with built-in safeguards against pathological setups and all-or-nothing error handling to prevent the recovery mechanism itself from crashing.

files.name: bridge/bridgeStatusUtil.ts
files.summary: This utility module provides UI formatting and state management helpers for a bridge connection status display. It exports constants for animation timing, functions to construct connection URLs and status labels based on connection state, and specialized text-rendering utilities (including shimmer animation logic and terminal hyperlink formatting) that handle multi-byte characters and emoji correctly. Essentially, it centralizes the presentation logic for showing users the bridge's connection status and related UI elements.

files.name: bridge/bridgeUI.ts
files.summary: This file creates a terminal UI logger for displaying the status of a remote control bridge session. It manages the rendering of status lines, QR codes, session information, and connection states—handling layout concerns like line wrapping, cursor positioning, and visual updates while abstracting the underlying terminal control codes from callers. The logger tracks connection state (idle, connecting, attached, reconnecting, failed), displays repository and branch info, manages multi-session capacity indicators, and provides methods for logging session events and toggling the QR code display.

files.name: bridge/capacityWake.ts
files.summary: This module provides a reusable capacity-wake mechanism that bridges poll loops use to sleep efficiently while at capacity and wake up when either the system shuts down or capacity becomes available. It merges two abort signals (outer shutdown signal and an internal capacity controller) into a single signal, allowing poll loops to wait on one signal rather than duplicating this logic in multiple places.

files.name: bridge/codeSessionApi.ts
files.summary: This file provides lightweight HTTP clients for the Anthropic CCR v2 code-session API, enabling callers to create remote code sessions and fetch worker credentials. It's intentionally kept separate and minimal—exporting only two functions without bundling heavy dependencies like CLI tools or analytics—so the SDK's `/bridge` subpath can be imported independently by callers who supply their own authentication tokens and base URLs.

files.name: bridge/createSession.ts
files.summary: This file provides HTTP client functions for managing remote control sessions on a bridge environment via a Sessions API. It handles creating new sessions (optionally pre-populated with conversation history and git repository context), fetching session metadata, archiving sessions on shutdown, and syncing title updates back to the server. All functions authenticate using OAuth tokens scoped to the user's organization and gracefully degrade on errors since session management is best-effort.

files.name: bridge/debugUtils.ts
files.summary: This file provides utility functions for safe debug logging and error handling in the bridge layer. It redacts sensitive fields (tokens, secrets) from logged data, truncates large payloads to prevent log bloat, and extracts human-readable error messages from HTTP responses. It also centralizes analytics event logging for bridge initialization skips to avoid boilerplate across call sites.

files.name: bridge/envLessBridgeConfig.ts
files.summary: This file defines the configuration schema and runtime parameters for the "env-less" (v2) bridge—a newer implementation of Remote Control sessions. It provides typed defaults for timing behaviors (retries, timeouts, heartbeats, token refresh), deduplication buffers, and version gating, fetches the actual config values from GrowthBook feature flags with strict validation, and exposes helpers to check CLI version compatibility and determine whether to prompt users for app upgrades. The validation is deliberately strict, rejecting partially invalid configs entirely to fall back to safe defaults rather than mixing trusted and untrusted values.

files.name: bridge/flushGate.ts
files.summary: FlushGate manages a state machine that queues outgoing messages during the initial sync of historical data to the server, ensuring new messages don't interleave with the historical batch. It provides lifecycle methods to start the flush (begin queueing), end it (retrieve queued items), drop queued items on connection failure, or deactivate without dropping (when the transport is replaced and a new one will handle draining).

files.name: bridge/inboundAttachments.ts
files.summary: This file handles file attachments sent from the web composer to Claude by downloading them from a secure API endpoint, saving them locally, and injecting file references into the message content. It implements a best-effort approach where network or disk failures are logged as debug messages but don't block message delivery to Claude—the message simply proceeds without the attachment references if resolution fails.

files.name: bridge/inboundMessages.ts
files.summary: This file handles normalization of user messages received from bridge clients, particularly fixing image content blocks that use incorrect field naming conventions. It extracts message content and UUIDs for processing, and corrects camelCase `mediaType` fields to the required snake_case `media_type` format to prevent downstream API failures. The code includes a fast-path optimization to avoid unnecessary allocations when messages don't need normalization.

files.name: bridge/initReplBridge.ts
files.summary: This file initializes the bridge connection for the REPL environment by reading bootstrap state (OAuth tokens, organization policy, session metadata, git context) and delegating to the core bridge logic. It decides between two connection paths—environment-based (v1, using poll/ack/heartbeat) or environment-less (v2, direct worker connection)—after validating prerequisites like authentication, org policy, and CLI version. It also owns session title derivation (from explicit names, storage, or conversation content) and fires-and-forgets title refinement via Haiku generation at message counts 1 and 3. The file is split out separately to avoid pulling sessionStorage into the Agent SDK bundle when only the core bootstrap-free logic is needed.

files.name: bridge/jwtUtils.ts
files.summary: This file implements JWT token refresh scheduling for session management. It decodes JWT expiry claims and creates a scheduler that proactively refreshes session tokens before they expire by calling a provided callback with a new OAuth token—used by both standalone and REPL bridges to keep long-running sessions authenticated. The scheduler handles edge cases like stale in-flight refreshes, transient failures with retry logic, and follow-up refreshes for extended sessions.

files.name: bridge/pollConfig.ts
files.summary: This file defines the validation schema and fetching logic for the bridge's polling interval configuration, which controls how frequently the system checks for work across different capacity states. It retrieves a remotely-managed configuration from GrowthBook with strict validation rules (minimum intervals, required liveness mechanisms) to prevent operational mistakes like accidental tight-loops, and falls back to sensible defaults if the config is missing or invalid. The configuration is shared across both standalone and REPL bridge deployments so operations can tune polling behavior fleet-wide in one place.

files.name: bridge/pollConfigDefaults.ts
files.summary: This file defines default polling intervals and configuration values for the bridge's work-polling mechanism, isolated to avoid unnecessary dependencies on dynamic configuration systems. It specifies how frequently the bridge checks for new work when idle versus when at capacity, along with multisession variants and related keepalive parameters that operations teams can override via GrowthBook feature flags.

files.name: bridge/remoteBridgeCore.ts
files.summary:
# remoteBridgeCore.ts

This file implements an environment-less Remote Control bridge that connects directly to Anthropic's code execution session API without the legacy Environments API layer. It orchestrates a multi-step initialization flow: creating a code session via OAuth, fetching worker credentials, establishing a v2 transport (SSE + CCR protocol), and managing JWT refresh/recovery cycles. The core enables REPL interactions (message writes, control requests, teardown) while handling authentication failures, echo deduplication, and graceful shutdown within process exit budgets.

files.name: bridge/replBridgeHandle.ts
files.summary: This file maintains a global reference to the active REPL bridge handle, allowing tools and slash commands outside the React component tree to invoke bridge methods like subscribing to pull requests. It provides getter/setter functions and publishes the bridge's session ID to a shared session record so other local peers can identify and avoid duplicating this instance.

files.name: bridge/replBridgeTransport.ts
files.summary: This file provides a transport abstraction layer for replBridge that unifies two different backend protocols (v1 Session-Ingress and v2 CCR) behind a single `ReplBridgeTransport` interface. The v1 adapter wraps HybridTransport as-is, while the v2 adapter orchestrates SSETransport for reading events and CCRClient for writing events, handling worker registration, epoch management, and delivery acknowledgment tracking. It exists to isolate the protocol-version choice from replBridge's core logic, allowing both paths to coexist transparently.

files.name: bridge/sessionIdCompat.ts
files.summary: This file provides session ID tag translation helpers for bridging between two API versions in a CCR v2 compatibility layer. It converts session IDs between `cse_*` (infrastructure/worker format) and `session_*` (client-facing compat API format) to ensure the same underlying UUID is tagged correctly depending on which endpoint is being called. The functions live separately from other session utilities to avoid pulling unnecessary dependencies into the SDK bundle, and include a kill switch gate to conditionally enable the shim based on a GrowthBook feature flag.

files.name: bridge/sessionRunner.ts
files.summary: This file spawns and manages child CLI processes that run Claude Code sessions, handling their lifecycle, I/O streams, and communication with the bridge. It parses NDJSON output from the child process to extract and track session activities (tool invocations, text, results), forwards permission requests to the server, and provides methods to control the process (send input, update auth tokens, gracefully or forcefully terminate). The spawner also captures debug logs and transcripts for post-hoc analysis when needed.

files.name: bridge/trustedDevice.ts
files.summary: This module manages trusted device tokens for elevated-security bridge (remote-control) sessions. It handles enrollment via a server endpoint to obtain a persistent token, retrieval of stored tokens with caching, and clearing tokens during logout or account switches. The feature is gated server-side and CLI-side to enable staged rollout, and all operations are best-effort to avoid blocking the login flow.

files.name: bridge/types.ts
files.summary: This file defines the core TypeScript types and constants for the bridge—the local component that connects `claude remote-control` to Claude's backend environments API. It establishes the protocol contracts for work polling, session management, permissions, and configuration, while also defining dependency interfaces for API clients, session spawning, and logging that allow the bridge to be testable and pluggable.

files.name: bridge/workSecret.ts
files.summary: This module handles work secret decoding and session URL/registration for the bridge to communicate with Claude Code Runner (CCR) v2 infrastructure. It provides utilities to decode base64url-encoded secrets containing API credentials, build WebSocket and HTTP URLs for session ingress, compare session IDs across different tagging schemes used by v1/v2 compatibility layers, and register the bridge as a worker with the remote session service.

files.name: buddy/CompanionSprite.tsx
files.summary:
# CompanionSprite.tsx

This file renders an interactive companion character that appears in the terminal UI, displaying animations, speech bubbles, and responding to user interactions like petting. It manages the companion's idle animations, reaction states (with fade-out timing), and adapts its display based on terminal width—showing either a full sprite with speech bubble or a compact single-line face depending on available space.

files.name: buddy/companion.ts
files.summary: This file implements a deterministic companion character generator that creates persistent "buddy" characters based on user IDs. It uses seeded random generation to ensure the same user always gets the same character appearance and stats, while allowing character attributes (species, rarity, eyes, hats, stats) to be regenerated from stored configuration without persisting the actual generated values. The caching and salt-based hashing ensure efficient generation across frequent calls from UI updates and game ticks.

files.name: buddy/prompt.ts
files.summary: This file generates introductory context for a companion character feature that appears alongside the user in the chat interface. The `companionIntroText` function creates instructions for the AI to understand its role when a named companion is present (staying out of the way when the companion is addressed directly), while `getCompanionIntroAttachment` decides whether to include this introduction in the conversation by checking if the companion feature is enabled, not muted, and hasn't already been introduced in the current message thread.

files.name: buddy/sprites.ts
files.summary: This file defines ASCII art sprites for various companion characters and provides functions to render them. It stores multiple animation frames per species (with subtle variations for idle fidgeting), supports customizable eyes and hats, and exports utilities to render the complete sprite or extract just the face representation for different display contexts.

files.name: buddy/types.ts
files.summary: This file defines the type system and constants for a "companion" or "buddy" system—a feature that generates personalized virtual creatures with randomized attributes like species, rarity level, and cosmetic traits. The species names are encoded as character codes to evade build-time string scanning, while the type definitions separate deterministic properties (bones derived from user hash) from persistent customization data (soul), enabling the system to regenerate creature appearance without breaking stored user data.

files.name: buddy/useBuddyNotification.tsx
files.summary: This file manages notifications and UI elements for a "Buddy" feature—a companion system with a limited-time teaser window (April 1-7, 2026) and permanent activation afterward. It provides utility functions to check if the feature is in teaser or live mode, renders rainbow-colored text for visual appeal, displays a dismissible notification prompting users to try the `/buddy` command on startup (if no companion exists yet), and identifies positions of `/buddy` triggers in text for parsing.

files.name: cli/exit.ts
files.summary: This module provides standardized exit helpers (`cliError` and `cliOk`) for CLI subcommand handlers to eliminate repetitive boilerplate. It consolidates the common pattern of printing a message, suppressing linter warnings, and exiting the process into two reusable functions with `never` return types that help TypeScript understand control flow without requiring explicit returns at call sites.

files.name: cli/handlers/agents.ts
files.summary: This file handles the `claude agents` CLI command, which discovers and displays all configured agents in the current project. It loads agent definitions with their overrides, filters for active agents, and prints them grouped by source with details like model and memory configuration, while noting which agents are shadowed by overrides from higher-priority sources.

files.name: cli/handlers/auth.ts
files.summary: This file implements CLI handlers for authentication operations: login, logout, and status checking. It orchestrates OAuth flows (supporting both claude.ai and Console authentication), token management, and account information retrieval, while also supporting environment variable-based token exchange for programmatic access. The handlers are responsible for persisting credentials, fetching user roles and profile data, validating organizational constraints, and reporting authentication state to the user.

files.name: cli/handlers/autoMode.ts
files.summary: This file provides CLI handlers for managing and reviewing Claude Code's auto mode classifier rules. It supports three commands: displaying default classifier rules, showing the effective merged configuration (user rules overriding defaults), and using an LLM to critique user-written rules for clarity and potential issues. The critique handler is particularly useful for helping users write better rules by analyzing them against the full classifier system prompt.

files.name: cli/handlers/plugins.ts
files.summary: This file provides CLI command handlers for the plugin and marketplace management system. It exports handler functions for commands like `plugin install`, `plugin list`, `marketplace add`, etc., and is dynamically imported only when users run plugin-related commands to avoid loading unnecessary code at startup. The handlers orchestrate plugin lifecycle operations (install, uninstall, enable, disable, update), marketplace configuration (add, list, remove, update), plugin validation, and output formatting for both human-readable and JSON modes, while also logging analytics events for each command.

files.name: cli/handlers/util.tsx
files.summary: This file exports three CLI subcommand handlers extracted for lazy loading: `setupTokenHandler`, `doctorHandler`, and `installHandler`. Each handler renders a React component tree with providers (app state, keybindings, MCP connection management) and logs analytics events before exiting the process. The handlers facilitate token setup via OAuth flow, system diagnostics with plugin management, and package installation respectively.

files.name: cli/ndjsonSafeStringify.ts
files.summary: This file provides a safe JSON stringification function for NDJSON (newline-delimited JSON) streams that prevents data corruption caused by Unicode line terminators. JSON.stringify can emit U+2028 and U+2029 characters which JavaScript treats as line breaks, causing receivers that split on line terminators to truncate messages mid-string; this utility escapes those characters into safe `\uXXXX` sequences while keeping the output valid JSON that parses identically.

files.name: cli/remoteIO.ts
files.summary: RemoteIO manages bidirectional streaming communication for SDK mode by establishing a remote connection (WebSocket or SSE) to a session ingress service, handling authentication tokens, and coordinating message flow with optional CCR v2 client support for heartbeats and state reporting. It bridges local input/output streams with remote transports while providing keep-alive pings for bridge deployments and graceful connection cleanup.

files.name: cli/structuredIO.ts
files.summary: This file manages structured bidirectional communication between the CLI and an SDK host (like VS Code) over stdin/stdout, handling tool permission requests and other control messages. It parses incoming NDJSON messages, routes permission decisions through hooks and SDK prompts, and serializes outgoing control requests—particularly enabling the SDK host to approve or deny tool execution, sandbox network access, and callback invocations. The class also deduplicates late-arriving responses and prevents duplicate tool execution by tracking resolved requests.

files.name: cli/transports/HybridTransport.ts
files.summary: HybridTransport implements a dual-channel message delivery system that uses WebSocket for reads and HTTP POST for writes, optimizing throughput by batching stream events and serializing writes to prevent concurrent Firestore collisions. It buffers rapid `stream_event` messages for up to 100ms before posting, delegates serialization and retry logic to a background uploader, and applies backpressure to prevent queue overflow—ensuring fire-and-forget callers don't block while still allowing awaiting callers to confirm persistence.

files.name: cli/transports/SSETransport.ts
files.summary: SSETransport implements a bidirectional communication channel that reads events from a server via Server-Sent Events (SSE) and sends messages back via HTTP POST. It handles automatic reconnection with exponential backoff, duplicate detection via sequence numbering, and liveness monitoring to detect dead connections. The transport parses incoming SSE frames to extract client events and forwards their payloads to consumers, while providing reliable message delivery with retry logic for outgoing POST requests.

files.name: cli/transports/SerialBatchEventUploader.ts
files.summary: This file implements a robust event batching uploader that sends items to a server serially while managing backpressure, retries, and failures. It exists to provide reliable event delivery with features like configurable batch sizing (by count and byte limit), exponential backoff with jitter on failures, support for server-supplied retry hints (Retry-After), and the ability to drop problematic batches after too many consecutive failures to prevent permanent queue stalls.

files.name: cli/transports/WebSocketTransport.ts
files.summary: WebSocketTransport manages bidirectional WebSocket communication with resilient reconnection and message durability. It abstracts over both Bun's native WebSocket and Node's `ws` package, automatically reconnects on failure with exponential backoff and sleep detection, buffers outgoing messages by UUID for replay after reconnection, and maintains connection health through periodic pings and keep-alive frames to prevent proxy idle-timeout drops. It also logs detailed telemetry and diagnostic events for monitoring, particularly for remote control sessions running on serverless platforms like Cloudflare.

files.name: cli/transports/WorkerStateUploader.ts
files.summary: This class manages batched uploads of worker session state to a server endpoint, combining multiple rapid updates into single requests to reduce network traffic. It implements a coalescing queue with exponential backoff retry logic—multiple enqueued patches are merged together using RFC 7396 rules (last value wins at top level, nested metadata objects are merged), and failed uploads automatically retry with increasing delays until success or explicit closure. The design naturally bounds concurrent requests at two (one in-flight, one pending) without requiring explicit backpressure handling.

files.name: cli/transports/ccrClient.ts
files.summary: This file implements the CCR (Claude Code Runtime) v2 client, which manages a worker's lifecycle and communication with a cloud session backend. It handles worker registration via epoch-based identity, periodic heartbeat liveness detection, and batched event uploads (client events, internal transcript events, and delivery status). It also implements text-delta coalescing for streaming messages—buffering partial content updates and emitting full-so-far snapshots so mid-stream clients receive self-contained chunks rather than fragments. Authentication, 409 epoch mismatch handling, and exponential backoff on retries are built in.

files.name: cli/transports/transportUtils.ts
files.summary: This utility selects the appropriate network transport mechanism for communicating with a backend service based on environment configuration and URL protocol. It implements a priority-based strategy to choose between three transport types: SSETransport (for server-sent events with HTTP POST writes), HybridTransport (WebSocket reads with HTTP POST writes), or WebSocketTransport (pure WebSocket), with the choice driven by feature flags and protocol detection. It also handles protocol conversion when needed (e.g., converting `wss:` to `https:` for SSE endpoints).

files.name: cli/update.ts
files.summary: This CLI command handles checking for and installing updates to the Claude application. It first diagnoses the current installation type (native, local npm, global npm, package manager, or development), displays any warnings or configuration mismatches, then performs the appropriate update procedure—whether that's using the native installer, npm registries, or directing users to their package manager. It also regenerates shell completion caches after successful updates and provides detailed error messages with troubleshooting steps when updates fail.

files.name: commands.ts
files.summary: This file serves as the central registry and loader for all commands in the application. It imports dozens of built-in commands, conditionally loads feature-flagged commands, and provides utility functions to retrieve, filter, and search commands based on availability, enablement, user authentication, and execution context (remote mode, bridge mode, etc.). The file also manages skills from multiple sources—bundled, plugin, skill directories, and workflows—combining them into a unified command list that the rest of the application queries.

files.name: commands/add-dir/add-dir.tsx
files.summary:
# add-dir.tsx

Implements a CLI command that allows users to add workspace directories to their working environment. It handles both interactive directory selection (when no path is provided) and direct path input, validates the directory, applies permission updates to both the session and optional persistent storage, and refreshes the sandbox configuration to make new directories accessible to bash commands.

files.name: commands/add-dir/index.ts
files.summary: This file defines and exports a command metadata object that registers the "add-dir" command, which allows users to add a new working directory to the application. The metadata specifies the command's type, name, description, expected arguments, and how to lazy-load its implementation.

files.name: commands/add-dir/validation.ts
files.summary: This file validates and reports on attempts to add a directory to the workspace's accessible paths. It checks whether a given directory path exists, is actually a directory, and isn't already covered by an existing working directory, then returns a detailed result object that describes success or the specific reason for failure. The companion `addDirHelpMessage` function translates these validation results into user-friendly error or success messages.

files.name: commands/advisor.ts
files.summary: This file implements the `/advisor` command, which allows users to configure a secondary AI model that provides guidance alongside the main conversation model. It handles enabling/disabling the advisor, validating that the selected model supports the advisor role, and persisting the user's choice to settings while providing feedback on compatibility with the currently active main model.

files.name: commands/agents/agents.tsx
files.summary: This file implements a command that displays the agents menu interface. It retrieves the available tools based on the current application's permission context and renders the AgentsMenu component with those tools, allowing users to interact with agents and exit via the provided callback.

files.name: commands/agents/index.ts
files.summary: This file exports a command definition that registers the agents management tool with the CLI system, allowing users to view and modify agent configurations through a local JSX-based command interface.

files.name: commands/ant-trace/index.js
files.summary: This is a stub implementation of the ant-trace command that is permanently disabled and hidden from the user interface. It appears to be a placeholder, possibly left in the codebase for future development or as a remnant of a command that was removed or deprecated.

files.name: commands/autofix-pr/index.js
files.summary: This is a stub implementation of the autofix-pr command that is permanently disabled and hidden from the user interface. It appears to be a placeholder for a command feature that either hasn't been fully implemented or has been intentionally deactivated.

files.name: commands/backfill-sessions/index.js
files.summary: This file provides a disabled stub command for backfilling sessions. The command is hidden from the user interface and always returns false when checked for enablement, suggesting it's a placeholder for functionality that is either not yet implemented or intentionally deactivated.

files.name: commands/branch/branch.ts
files.summary: This file implements the branch command, which creates a fork of the current conversation into a new independent session. It copies the entire message history from the original transcript while preserving metadata (timestamps, git state, etc.), assigns a new session ID, and automatically generates a descriptive title—either from a user-provided name or derived from the first message. The fork is then immediately resumed so the user continues in the branched conversation, with a hint to resume the original if needed.

files.name: commands/branch/index.ts
files.summary: Defines a CLI command that allows users to create a branch (or fork) of the current conversation at a specific point. The command conditionally includes a 'fork' alias only when the separate fork subagent feature is disabled, and delegates its execution logic to a separate module.

files.name: commands/break-cache/index.js
files.summary: This file is a stub implementation of a cache-breaking command that is currently disabled and hidden from users. It appears to be a placeholder for future functionality or a deprecated feature that has been intentionally disabled rather than removed from the codebase.

files.name: commands/bridge-kick.ts
files.summary: This file implements a debug command (`/bridge-kick`) that allows developers to artificially inject failure scenarios into the bridge communication layer, enabling manual testing of recovery and reconnection logic. It's restricted to internal "ant" users and provides subcommands to simulate various failure modes like websocket closures, poll errors, registration failures, and heartbeat issues—each with detailed logging to observe how the system handles and recovers from these faults.

files.name: commands/bridge/bridge.tsx
files.summary: This file implements the `/remote-control` command, which manages bidirectional bridge connections between the CLI and claude.ai for remote code execution. When invoked, it performs prerequisite checks (org policy, version compatibility, authentication), then either shows a disconnect dialog if already connected, or enables the bridge by setting app state flags that trigger the actual connection logic elsewhere. The disconnect dialog displays the session URL, optionally generates a QR code, and allows users to disconnect, show the QR, or continue the session.

files.name: commands/bridge/index.ts
files.summary: Defines a CLI command that enables remote-control sessions through a bridge connection, conditionally available only when the `BRIDGE_MODE` feature flag is enabled and bridge functionality is configured. The command is lazily loaded and provides aliases like `rc` for user convenience.

files.name: commands/brief.ts
files.summary: This file implements a `/brief` slash command that toggles between brief-only mode (where all output must use a dedicated Brief tool) and normal mode. It gates command visibility and user entitlement via feature flags and GrowthBook configuration, logs toggle events for analytics, and injects system reminders to keep the model aware of mode changes mid-conversation.

files.name: commands/btw/btw.tsx
files.summary: This file implements the `/btw` command, a side-question feature that lets users ask Claude quick questions without interrupting the main conversation. It renders a React component that fetches answers via `runSideQuestion`, displays them in a scrollable panel with a loading spinner, and handles keyboard navigation (arrow keys to scroll, escape/enter/space to dismiss). The component also manages prompt cache optimization by reusing the last cached system/user/system context bytes when available to guarantee consistent cache hits on the AI backend.

files.name: commands/btw/index.ts
files.summary: This file registers a "btw" (by the way) command that allows users to pose quick side questions without disrupting the main conversation flow. It's a command metadata declaration that points to the actual implementation in `btw.js` and configures it to execute immediately with a user-provided question as input.

files.name: commands/bughunter/index.js
files.summary: This file is a stub placeholder for the bughunter command that is currently disabled and hidden from users. It appears to reserve a command name space for future implementation or to prevent conflicts with a deprecated/removed feature.

files.name: commands/chrome/chrome.tsx
files.summary: This file provides a UI menu for managing the "Claude in Chrome" extension, a feature that lets Claude Code control the browser directly. It handles extension installation detection, connection status display, permission management, and allows users to toggle whether the feature is enabled by default. The component also enforces prerequisites like Claude.ai subscription status and WSL compatibility.

files.name: commands/chrome/index.ts
files.summary: Defines a CLI command that provides access to Claude in Chrome settings, available only in interactive sessions. The command is registered as a local JSX handler that dynamically loads its implementation from a separate module.

files.name: commands/clear/caches.ts
files.summary: This module provides a comprehensive cache-clearing function for session management. It orchestrates clearing dozens of distributed caches across the codebase—including context, files, skills, git state, LSP diagnostics, and tool data—to ensure fresh discovery when resuming or restarting a session. The function supports preserving certain agent-scoped state during `/clear` operations while wiping everything else, and uses dynamic imports for feature-flagged or heavy modules to keep its startup cost minimal.

files.name: commands/clear/clear.ts
files.summary: Implements the `clear` command handler that wipes the current conversation state and returns an empty response. It delegates the actual clearing logic to a separate conversation module, providing a clean entry point for users to reset their chat history.

files.name: commands/clear/conversation.ts
files.summary: This module implements the `/clear` command, which resets a conversation session while preserving background tasks and their state. It orchestrates clearing messages, regenerating the session ID, cleaning up foreground tasks, resetting caches and app state, and re-executing session initialization hooks to provide a fresh conversational context without interrupting long-running background operations.

files.name: commands/clear/index.ts
files.summary: This file defines a lightweight command descriptor for a "clear" command that resets conversation state. The actual implementation is lazy-loaded on demand to improve startup performance, while this index serves only to register the command's metadata (name, description, aliases, and loading behavior).

files.name: commands/color/color.ts
files.summary: This command allows users to change the visual color of their agent session, with support for resetting to default. It validates the color choice against a predefined list, persists the selection to session storage for use across restarts, and updates the app state immediately for live visual feedback—while preventing teammates in a swarm from changing their own color.

files.name: commands/color/index.ts
files.summary: This file defines the metadata and lazy-loading entry point for the color command, which allows users to change the prompt bar color during a session. The actual command logic is deferred and loaded only when needed, reducing initial startup overhead.

files.name: commands/commit-push-pr.ts
files.summary: This command automates the full pull request workflow—committing staged changes, pushing to a branch, and creating or updating a PR on GitHub. It generates detailed instructions for an AI assistant, specifying allowed git and GitHub CLI commands, safety guardrails (like preventing force pushes to main), and optional Slack notifications. The command also supports special "undercover" mode for internal Anthropic users and includes attribution tracking for commits and PRs.

files.name: commands/commit.ts
files.summary: This file defines a git commit command that uses an AI prompt to intelligently create commits based on repository context. It instructs the AI to analyze staged changes, follow the repository's commit style conventions from recent commit history, and generate an appropriate commit message—while enforcing safety guardrails like preventing amended commits, avoiding secret files, and refusing interactive git operations. The command provides the AI with current git status, diffs, and branch information to make informed decisions about what to commit and how to describe it.

files.name: commands/compact/compact.ts
files.summary: This file implements the `/compact` command, which summarizes conversation history to reduce token usage when the context window fills up. It orchestrates three compaction strategies in priority order: session memory compaction (fastest, lightweight), reactive compaction (streaming-aware), and traditional compaction (fallback), while handling pre/post-compaction hooks, cache invalidation, and error states.

files.name: commands/compact/index.ts
files.summary: This file defines and exports a command metadata object for a "compact" feature that allows users to clear conversation history while preserving a summary. The command can be toggled via an environment variable and supports optional custom instructions for how the summarization should be performed.

files.name: commands/config/config.tsx
files.summary: This file implements a command that displays the application's configuration settings interface. It exports a `call` function that renders the `Settings` component with the "Config" tab pre-selected, allowing users to access and modify application configuration through a dedicated UI dialog.

files.name: commands/config/index.ts
files.summary: Registers a command named "config" that opens a configuration panel, with "settings" as an alias. The command is defined as a local JSX component that lazily loads its implementation from a separate module, allowing users to access and modify application settings.

files.name: commands/context/context-noninteractive.ts
files.summary: This file implements the `/context` slash command and SDK `get_context_usage` request, which displays a detailed breakdown of token usage across the conversation context. It collects and analyzes how tokens are distributed among messages, tools, agents, memory files, skills, and other components, then formats the results as a markdown table. The analysis mirrors the exact transformations (message compacting, context collapse, microcompact) that the model sees, ensuring the reported token counts match what's actually sent to the API.

files.name: commands/context/context.tsx
files.summary: This file implements the `/context` command, which displays what the AI model actually sees when processing messages. It transforms the raw message history through the same compaction and context collapse steps that occur before API calls, then analyzes and visualizes the token usage and message structure so users understand the exact context being sent to the model rather than seeing inaccurate counts based on the REPL's unprocessed history.

files.name: commands/context/index.ts
files.summary: This file exports two command variants for visualizing token/context usage: an interactive JSX-based visualization for regular sessions, and a non-interactive text-based version for headless environments. Each command is conditionally enabled based on whether the session supports interactivity, allowing the CLI to provide appropriate output formats depending on the execution context.

files.name: commands/copy/copy.tsx
files.summary:
# Copy Command

This file implements a `/copy` command that lets users copy assistant responses or extracted code blocks to their clipboard and optionally to a temp file as a fallback. It provides an interactive picker UI when multiple code blocks are available, allowing users to choose what to copy (full response, individual code blocks, or always copy full). The command also supports keyboard shortcuts (Enter to copy, W to write to file, Esc to cancel) and tracks usage via analytics.

files.name: commands/copy/index.ts
files.summary: This file defines a lazy-loaded command that copies Claude's last response to the clipboard, with optional indexing to retrieve earlier responses. It uses a deferred loading pattern to minimize startup overhead by only importing the actual implementation when the command is invoked.

files.name: commands/cost/cost.ts
files.summary: This file implements a `/cost` command that displays Claude API usage and billing information to the user. It checks whether the user is a Claude AI subscriber and returns different messages—subscribers see their current plan status (subscription or overage), while non-subscribers see their total formatted costs. ANT-flagged users additionally see the raw cost breakdown regardless of subscription status.

files.name: commands/cost/index.ts
files.summary: This file defines the metadata for a "cost" command that displays session expenses and duration, with lazy-loading of the actual implementation to improve startup performance. The command visibility is dynamically controlled—hidden from Claude AI subscribers unless they're "Ants" (a special user type that always sees cost breakdowns)—and supports non-interactive execution.

files.name: commands/createMovedToPluginCommand.ts
files.summary: This file creates a placeholder command that redirects users to an external plugin when a built-in command has been moved out of the main codebase. It handles two scenarios: for internal users (ant), it provides direct plugin installation instructions, while for external users during the marketplace's private phase, it delegates to a custom prompt handler. This allows graceful deprecation of commands by guiding users to their new plugin locations.

files.name: commands/ctx_viz/index.js
files.summary: This file exports a stub command definition that is disabled and hidden from user access. It appears to be a placeholder or disabled feature in the command registry, possibly serving as a template or deactivated module that prevents the actual command from loading.

files.name: commands/debug-tool-call/index.js
files.summary: This is a stub command definition that is permanently disabled and hidden from the UI, likely serving as a placeholder or disabled feature in the codebase. It appears to be used to reserve a command name without actually enabling any functionality for it.

files.name: commands/desktop/desktop.tsx
files.summary: This file exports a command handler that renders a `DesktopHandoff` component, which appears to facilitate handing off work or context from a web interface to a desktop application. The async `call` function serves as the entry point for this command within the application's command system, accepting a callback to notify when the handoff is complete.

files.name: commands/desktop/index.ts
files.summary: Defines the `desktop` command metadata that enables users to continue their current session in Claude Desktop, with platform-specific availability restricted to macOS and 64-bit Windows. This command declaration registers the feature in the CLI's command system and dynamically loads the actual implementation only when needed on supported platforms.

files.name: commands/diff/diff.tsx
files.summary: This file exports a command handler that dynamically loads and renders a DiffDialog component. It serves as the entry point for a "diff" command, receiving message context and a completion callback, then displays a modal dialog to present differences between items.

files.name: commands/diff/index.ts
files.summary: Defines the metadata and registration entry point for a `diff` command that allows users to view uncommitted changes and per-turn diffs. The command is lazily loaded from a separate module and configured as a local JSX-based command within the CLI framework.

files.name: commands/doctor/doctor.tsx
files.summary: This file serves as a command entry point that instantiates and returns the Doctor screen component when invoked. It implements the LocalJSXCommandCall interface, passing through the completion callback to the Doctor component so the application can respond when the doctor operation finishes.

files.name: commands/doctor/index.ts
files.summary: This file registers the "doctor" command, which diagnoses and verifies Claude Code installations and settings. The command can be disabled via an environment variable and is implemented as a local JSX command that loads its logic from a separate module.

files.name: commands/effort/effort.tsx
files.summary:
# Summary

This file implements the `/effort` command, which allows users to control the effort level (low, medium, high, max, or auto) that determines how thoroughly the AI approaches code tasks. It handles command parsing, validates user input, persists settings to storage, and manages conflicts when environment variables override user preferences, while also tracking usage via analytics.

files.name: commands/effort/index.ts
files.summary: This file defines a CLI command that allows users to configure the effort level for model inference, with options ranging from low to max or automatic selection. It's a command definition that delegates to a separate implementation file and determines whether execution should happen immediately based on inference configuration settings.

files.name: commands/env/index.js
files.summary: This file appears to be a stub implementation of an environment command that is intentionally disabled and hidden from the user interface. It serves as a placeholder, likely reserved for future implementation or to maintain a consistent command structure without exposing incomplete functionality.

files.name: commands/exit/exit.tsx
files.summary: This file implements the `/exit` command for an interactive CLI tool. It handles three exit scenarios: detaching from background tmux sessions (keeping the REPL alive for later reconnection), showing a confirmation flow when in a worktree session, or performing a graceful shutdown with a random goodbye message. The command consolidates all exit paths (including `/quit`, Ctrl+C, and Ctrl+D) through a single handler.

files.name: commands/exit/index.ts
files.summary: Defines the metadata and configuration for an `exit` command that terminates the REPL session, with an alias `quit` and immediate execution enabled. This declaration allows the command infrastructure to recognize and route exit requests without loading the full implementation until needed.

files.name: commands/export/export.tsx
files.summary: This file implements a command to export conversation messages to a text file. It handles two workflows: when a filename is provided as an argument, it directly writes to that file; otherwise, it renders an interactive dialog with a auto-generated filename based on the conversation's first user message and current timestamp. The file also includes utility functions to sanitize filenames and extract/format relevant metadata from the conversation history.

files.name: commands/export/index.ts
files.summary: Defines the metadata and registration entry point for an "export" command that allows users to save the current conversation to a file or clipboard. The command defers loading the actual implementation logic until needed via dynamic import.

files.name: commands/extra-usage/extra-usage-core.ts
files.summary: This file implements the core logic for the `/extra-usage` command, which helps users request or manage additional token usage for their Claude account. For team/enterprise members without billing access, it checks eligibility and submits a usage limit increase request to their admin; for users with billing access, it opens the Claude web interface's usage settings page directly.

files.name: commands/extra-usage/extra-usage-noninteractive.ts
files.summary: This module provides a non-interactive command interface for managing extra usage by wrapping the core extra-usage logic and converting its result into a text response suitable for automated or CLI contexts. It handles both message-based responses and browser-opening scenarios, formatting appropriate user guidance depending on whether a browser was successfully launched.

files.name: commands/extra-usage/extra-usage.tsx
files.summary: This file implements the `/extra-usage` command handler, which checks usage limits and either displays a message or prompts the user to log in with a new account. If the user proceeds with login, it updates the API key and reports the result back to the command context.

files.name: commands/extra-usage/index.ts
files.summary: This file exports two command variants for managing extra usage provisioning when resource limits are reached. It provides an interactive JSX-based command for normal sessions and a non-interactive command for scripted/automated sessions, both gated behind feature flags and permission checks. The actual command logic is lazily loaded from separate implementation files.

files.name: commands/fast/fast.tsx
files.summary:
# Fast Mode Command

This file implements a CLI command that allows users to toggle "Fast Mode" — a high-speed, premium-tier execution mode for the Claude Opus 4.6 model. It provides both an interactive dialog picker for toggling the setting and shortcut handlers for direct `fast on`/`fast off` commands, while managing state updates, pricing display, cooldown status, and analytics logging around the feature.

files.name: commands/fast/index.ts
files.summary: This file defines a command that toggles fast mode for Claude AI interactions. It serves as a command registry entry that controls whether fast mode (using a specific fast model) is available and visible to users, and determines when the command should execute immediately versus wait for user confirmation.

files.name: commands/feedback/feedback.tsx
files.summary: This file implements a command handler for a feedback component, serving as the entry point between the command system and the Feedback UI component. It exports a `call` function that the command framework invokes to render a feedback form, extracting user-provided arguments as initial description text and passing along context like abort signals and message history. The `renderFeedbackComponent` helper abstracts the component rendering logic to allow flexible reuse with different prop configurations.

files.name: commands/feedback/index.ts
files.summary: Defines a command configuration that enables users to submit feedback or bug reports about Claude Code, with conditional enablement based on deployment environment, privacy settings, and policy restrictions. The command is disabled in certain configurations (Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry backends, essential-traffic-only mode, and for internal users), and only loads when permitted by usage policies.

files.name: commands/files/files.ts
files.summary: This command lists all files currently loaded in the tool's context, displaying their relative paths to the current working directory. It serves as a quick way for users to see which files are available for the tool to read or reference during operations.

files.name: commands/files/index.ts
files.summary: Defines a command metadata object that registers a "files" command for listing files currently in context, restricted to users of type "ant" and loadable on-demand from a separate module.

files.name: commands/good-claude/index.js
files.summary: This is a stub command definition that is disabled and hidden from users. It appears to serve as a placeholder or disabled feature in the command system, with no active functionality.

files.name: commands/heapdump/heapdump.ts
files.summary: This command triggers a heap dump operation for memory diagnostics and returns the file paths of the generated heap and diagnostic outputs, or an error message if the dump fails.

files.name: commands/heapdump/index.ts
files.summary: This file registers a hidden developer command that dumps the JavaScript heap to the user's Desktop directory. It's a metadata definition that declares the command's properties (name, description, type) and lazily loads the actual implementation from a separate module when needed. The command supports non-interactive execution and is hidden from normal user-facing command listings.

files.name: commands/help/help.tsx
files.summary: This file implements a help command handler that renders the HelpV2 component. It receives a list of available commands as options and displays them in an interactive help interface, calling the `onDone` callback when the user closes the help panel.

files.name: commands/help/index.ts
files.summary: Registers and exports a help command that displays available commands to users. This command definition uses lazy loading to defer importing the actual help implementation until it's needed.

files.name: commands/hooks/hooks.tsx
files.summary: This file implements a command handler for the hooks configuration feature. It logs an analytics event, retrieves the list of available tools based on the user's permissions, and renders the HooksConfigMenu component with those tools so users can configure webhook settings.

files.name: commands/hooks/index.ts
files.summary: Registers a CLI command that lets users view hook configurations for tool events, delegating the actual implementation to a dynamically-loaded module.

files.name: commands/ide/index.ts
files.summary: Defines the command metadata and entry point for the IDE management command, which allows users to control IDE integrations and view their status. The command lazily loads its implementation from a separate module and supports an optional `open` argument to trigger IDE launching functionality.

files.name: commands/init-verifiers.ts
files.summary: This file defines a command that guides users through creating verifier skills—automated verification scripts for code changes. It walks users through detecting their project structure (web apps, APIs, CLI tools), setting up appropriate browser automation or testing tools (Playwright, Chrome DevTools, Tmux), gathering project-specific configuration (dev server commands, authentication details), and finally generating skill files in `.claude/skills/` that the Verify agent can discover and use. Essentially, it's an interactive initialization wizard that bridges the gap between a raw codebase and automated verification capabilities.

files.name: commands/init.ts
files.summary: This file defines the `/init` command, which guides users through setting up CLAUDE.md files and optional skills/hooks to document their codebase for Claude Code. It has two implementations: a legacy version that creates a single CLAUDE.md by analyzing the repo, and a newer, more structured version that lets users choose what to set up (project vs. personal files, skills, hooks) and conducts a multi-phase interview to gather codebase practices and preferences before generating minimal, well-targeted documentation. The command exists to reduce onboarding friction and ensure Claude Code operates effectively within project-specific conventions and workflows.

files.name: commands/install-github-app/ApiKeyStep.tsx
files.summary: This component renders an interactive terminal UI step for the GitHub App installation process that lets users choose how to provide an API key. It presents three options (use existing key, create OAuth token, or enter new key), handles navigation between options via keyboard input, and conditionally displays a masked text input field when the user selects the "new key" option. The component manages cursor state for the text input and applies visual styling to highlight the currently selected option.

files.name: commands/install-github-app/CheckExistingSecretStep.tsx
files.summary: This React component renders an interactive terminal UI step that handles the user's choice when an existing `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` secret is detected in repository secrets. It presents two options: reuse the existing secret or create a new one with a custom name, then captures the user's selection and optional input via keyboard navigation and text entry.

files.name: commands/install-github-app/CheckGitHubStep.tsx
files.summary: This React component renders a simple status message indicating that the GitHub CLI installation is being checked. It's part of the GitHub App installation workflow and serves as a user-facing indicator during the verification step of the installation process.

files.name: commands/install-github-app/ChooseRepoStep.tsx
files.summary: This component renders a terminal UI step for selecting a GitHub repository during the GitHub App installation flow. It allows users to either use their current repository (if detected) or enter a custom repository URL, with keyboard navigation between options and validation to prevent empty submissions.

files.name: commands/install-github-app/CreatingStep.tsx
files.summary: This component renders a progress tracker for the GitHub App installation workflow. It displays a series of sequential steps (like "Getting repository information," "Creating branch," "Setting up secret," etc.) and visually indicates which step is completed, in progress, or pending based on the current installation stage. The exact steps shown vary depending on whether the workflow is being skipped and whether an existing API secret is being reused.

files.name: commands/install-github-app/ErrorStep.tsx
files.summary: This component displays an error message screen for the GitHub App installation process. It renders the error message, an optional reason, step-by-step fix instructions, and a link to manual setup documentation—all within a bordered box asking the user to press any key to exit.

files.name: commands/install-github-app/ExistingWorkflowStep.tsx
files.summary: This component displays a prompt when an existing Claude GitHub workflow file is detected in a repository. It allows users to choose between three actions: updating the workflow file to the latest version, skipping the update while configuring only secrets, or exiting without making changes. The component presents this choice via a select menu alongside repository information and a helpful link to the latest workflow template.

files.name: commands/install-github-app/InstallAppStep.tsx
files.summary: This component renders an interactive terminal UI that guides users through installing the Claude GitHub App for a specific repository. It displays installation instructions, the app's direct URL, repository-specific details, and prompts the user to press Enter once installation is complete, while also providing a link to manual setup documentation for troubleshooting.

files.name: commands/install-github-app/OAuthFlowStep.tsx
files.summary:
# OAuthFlowStep Component

This React component manages the OAuth authentication flow for creating GitHub Actions API tokens. It orchestrates the entire user experience—from initiating OAuth login through Claude AI, handling manual code entry as a fallback, to displaying success or error states. The component maintains a state machine tracking various stages (starting, waiting for login, processing, success, error, retry) and integrates clipboard functionality to help users paste authorization codes when browser-based login fails.

files.name: commands/install-github-app/SuccessStep.tsx
files.summary: This component renders a success confirmation screen for the GitHub App installation process. It displays which secrets were created or reused, confirms whether a GitHub Actions workflow was generated, and provides conditional next-step instructions depending on whether the workflow was modified. The different messaging paths handle scenarios like using an existing API key secret versus creating a new one, and guide users through either merging a pre-filled PR or manually enabling the workflow.

files.name: commands/install-github-app/WarningsStep.tsx
files.summary: This component displays a list of setup warnings to the user during GitHub App installation, allowing them to review potential issues before proceeding. It renders warning titles, messages, and optional step-by-step instructions in a formatted box, with keyboard controls to continue (Enter) or exit (Ctrl+C), plus a link to manual setup documentation as an alternative.

files.name: commands/install-github-app/index.ts
files.summary: Defines a command that enables users to set up Claude GitHub Actions for a repository. The command is available in both Claude AI and console interfaces, can be disabled via environment variable, and dynamically loads its implementation when invoked.

files.name: commands/install-github-app/setupGitHubActions.ts
files.summary: This file orchestrates the setup of GitHub Actions workflows for the Claude code assistant in a user's repository. It handles creating workflow files (for PR assistance and code review), setting up authentication secrets, and opening a pull request for the user to review and merge. The function validates repository access, creates a feature branch, conditionally deploys selected workflows with proper secret substitution, and tracks setup success/failure through analytics.

files.name: commands/install-slack-app/index.ts
files.summary: This file defines a command metadata entry that registers the "install-slack-app" command with the CLI framework. It specifies that this is an interactive, local command available only in the Claude AI environment, and defers the actual implementation logic to a separate module that's loaded on-demand.

files.name: commands/install-slack-app/install-slack-app.ts
files.summary: This command facilitates installing the Claude Slack app by opening the Slack marketplace listing in the user's browser. It tracks installation attempts via analytics and increments a counter in the global config, then returns a success or fallback message depending on whether the browser opened successfully.

files.name: commands/install.tsx
files.summary:
# Summary

This file implements the CLI install command for Claude Code's native build. It orchestrates the complete installation workflow: downloading/installing the native binary, configuring shell integration and launcher setup, cleaning up legacy npm installations, and providing user feedback through a terminal UI. The component handles various installation states (checking, installing, setting-up, success, error) and logs telemetry events to track installation success.

files.name: commands/issue/index.js
files.summary: This file exports a stub command configuration that is disabled and hidden from the CLI. It appears to serve as a placeholder or disabled feature for an issue-related command, preventing it from being executed or displayed to users.

files.name: commands/keybindings/index.ts
files.summary: Registers a CLI command that allows users to open or create their custom keybindings configuration file, but only when keybinding customization is enabled in the system. The command is interactive-only and loads its implementation dynamically when invoked.

files.name: commands/keybindings/keybindings.ts
files.summary: This command enables users to create and customize keybindings by opening a keybindings configuration file in their default editor. It creates the file with a template if it doesn't exist, handles the case where the file already exists, and provides appropriate feedback on whether the file was created or just opened.

files.name: commands/login/index.ts
files.summary: This file defines a CLI login command that allows users to authenticate with Anthropic. It dynamically adjusts its description based on whether the user is already logged in (showing "Switch accounts" vs "Sign in"), can be disabled via environment variable, and lazily loads the actual login logic when invoked.

files.name: commands/login/login.tsx
files.summary: This file implements the login command for the CLI application. It orchestrates the OAuth authentication flow and handles all the side effects that occur after a successful login, including resetting cached state, refreshing remote settings and feature flags, clearing stale tokens, and bumping an auth version to trigger re-fetching of dependent data across the app. The UI presents a dialog with an embedded OAuth flow component and allows the user to cancel via keyboard shortcut.

files.name: commands/logout/index.ts
files.summary: Defines a CLI command that allows users to sign out from their Anthropic account. The command can be disabled via an environment variable and is implemented as a lazy-loaded JSX component, enabling dynamic loading of the logout functionality only when needed.

files.name: commands/logout/logout.tsx
files.summary: This file implements the logout command for the CLI, handling complete session termination and cleanup. It flushes telemetry data before clearing credentials, wipes secure storage, removes API keys, and invalidates all authentication-related caches (OAuth tokens, user data, betas, tool schemas, policy limits, and managed settings). When called, it performs full logout with onboarding reset and displays a success message before gracefully shutting down the application.

files.name: commands/mcp/addCommand.ts
files.summary: This file defines the `mcp add` CLI subcommand, which allows users to register new MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers with Claude Code. It handles three transport types (stdio, HTTP, and SSE), supports OAuth configuration, environment variables, and headers, and persists the server configuration to the appropriate scope file (local, user, or project). The command includes validation for required arguments, helpful warnings when users appear to misuse the transport type, and XAA identity federation support.

files.name: commands/mcp/index.ts
files.summary: Defines a command entry point that enables users to manage MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers through enable/disable operations, delegating the actual implementation to a separate mcp.js module for lazy loading.

files.name: commands/mcp/mcp.tsx
files.summary: This file implements the `/mcp` command handler, which provides a command-line interface for managing Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. It parses command arguments to route users to different MCP management screens: enabling/disabling servers (via the `MCPToggle` component), reconnecting to a specific server, viewing settings with a test bypass, or redirecting to the plugins interface. The `MCPToggle` component handles the actual toggling logic by filtering and toggling the appropriate servers based on the action and target parameters.

files.name: commands/mcp/xaaIdpCommand.ts
files.summary: This file implements CLI commands for managing XAA (SEP-990) identity provider connections in Claude Code's MCP system. It provides four subcommands: `setup` to configure an IdP with issuer URL and client credentials, `login` to cache an OIDC id_token for silent authentication across XAA-enabled servers, `show` to display the current configuration, and `clear` to remove it. The implementation carefully orchestrates settings persistence and keychain storage to avoid leaving the system in an inconsistent state, and includes validation to prevent configuration poisoning (e.g., rejecting non-HTTPS issuers except on loopback).

files.name: commands/memory/index.ts
files.summary: Registers a local command that allows users to edit Claude memory files, delegating the actual implementation to a separate memory.js module that loads on demand.

files.name: commands/memory/memory.tsx
files.summary: This file implements the memory command, which allows users to create and edit persistent memory files that Claude can reference across conversations. When invoked, it displays a dialog with a file selector, handles file creation/initialization in the Claude config directory, opens the selected file in the user's preferred text editor (via `$VISUAL` or `$EDITOR` environment variables), and provides feedback about which editor was used.

files.name: commands/mobile/index.ts
files.summary: Defines and exports a CLI command that displays a QR code for downloading the Claude mobile app, accessible via the `mobile`, `ios`, or `android` command names. This command definition lazy-loads its implementation from a separate module and registers it with the command system as a local JSX-based command.

files.name: commands/mobile/mobile.tsx
files.summary: This file implements a mobile app discovery command that displays QR codes for downloading Claude on iOS and Android. It generates both QR codes upfront to avoid visual flicker, allows users to switch between platforms using tab/arrow keys, and provides a keyboard-driven interface to close the view. The component renders the selected platform's QR code along with its app store URL.

files.name: commands/mock-limits/index.js
files.summary: This file defines a stub command that is disabled and hidden from the UI, serving as a placeholder or disabled mock for limit-related functionality. It appears to be part of a command registration system where commands can be selectively enabled or hidden.

files.name: commands/model/index.ts
files.summary: This file exports a command definition that allows users to set the AI model used by Claude Code. It dynamically generates the command's description to show the currently active model and determines whether the command should execute immediately based on inference configuration settings. The actual command logic is lazy-loaded from a separate module.

files.name: commands/model/model.tsx
files.summary:
# Model Command Handler

This file implements a `/model` command that allows users to view and change the AI model used for code generation. It provides three interaction modes: an interactive model picker when no arguments are given, inline model setting via `/model [modelName]`, and info display via `/model --help` or `/model --info`. The component handles model validation, enforces organizational allowlists, manages compatibility with features like fast mode, and displays pricing information for models billed as extra usage.

files.name: commands/oauth-refresh/index.js
files.summary: This file is a stub implementation of an OAuth refresh command that is intentionally disabled and hidden from users. It appears to be a placeholder, possibly reserved for future implementation or kept in the codebase to maintain a consistent command structure while the feature is not yet active.

files.name: commands/onboarding/index.js
files.summary: This file provides a disabled stub implementation of an onboarding command. It appears to be a placeholder that prevents an actual onboarding feature from being registered or executed, likely while that functionality is under development or intentionally deactivated.

files.name: commands/output-style/index.ts
files.summary: This file registers a deprecated command that previously allowed users to change output style, now redirecting them to use the `/config` command instead. It's marked as hidden since it's no longer the recommended approach, but kept in the codebase to maintain backward compatibility or provide a deprecation notice.

files.name: commands/output-style/output-style.tsx
