files.summary: This file implements a deprecated command handler that notifies users the `/output-style` command no longer exists, directing them to use `/config` or their settings file instead to change output style. It serves as a graceful deprecation message to guide users toward the replacement functionality.

files.name: commands/passes/index.ts
files.summary: This file defines a command that allows users to share free Claude Code passes with friends and earn rewards for referrals. It dynamically hides the command based on eligibility checks and conditionally displays reward information in the description if the user qualifies for referrer bonuses.

files.name: commands/passes/passes.tsx
files.summary: This file implements a command handler for the guest passes section of the application. It tracks when a user first visits the passes page by updating a configuration flag, captures the remaining pass count at that time, and logs an analytics event to monitor user engagement with the passes feature. The handler then renders the Passes component to display the actual UI.

files.name: commands/perf-issue/index.js
files.summary: This is a stub command that is intentionally disabled and hidden from the CLI interface. It appears to be a placeholder, possibly reserved for a future performance issue command or left in place as a non-functional skeleton to be implemented later.

files.name: commands/permissions/index.ts
files.summary: Declares a command metadata object for a permissions management tool that allows users to configure which tools are allowed or denied. The actual command logic is lazily loaded from a separate module when needed, following the framework's command registration pattern.

files.name: commands/permissions/permissions.tsx
files.summary: This file is a command handler that renders a permissions management interface. It exports a React component that displays the `PermissionRuleList` component, handling two key interactions: exiting the permissions view and retrying failed permission commands by appending retry messages to the application's message context.

files.name: commands/plan/index.ts
files.summary: This file defines and exports the metadata for a "plan" command that enables plan mode or displays the current session plan. It serves as a command registry entry that lazy-loads the actual command implementation, allowing the CLI to discover and invoke planning functionality with optional arguments to either open the plan or provide a description.

files.name: commands/plan/plan.tsx
files.summary: This file implements the `/plan` command, which manages a planning mode within the application. It handles three main flows: enabling plan mode if not already active, displaying the current plan with its file path and editor information, and opening the plan file in an external editor when the user specifies `/plan open`. The component uses React for rendering the plan display output and integrates with the app's permission system to transition into plan mode.

files.name: commands/plugin/AddMarketplace.tsx
files.summary: This React component provides a UI for adding new plugin marketplaces to the application. It accepts user input (GitHub repos, SSH URLs, local paths, or direct URLs), validates and parses the input, then integrates the marketplace into the system's configuration. The component supports both interactive mode (switching to a browse view after success) and CLI mode (returning a result string), and automatically triggers the add operation if an initial value is provided.

files.name: commands/plugin/PluginErrors.tsx
files.summary: This file provides error formatting and guidance for plugin-related failures. It exports two functions that transform `PluginError` objects into human-readable messages: `formatErrorMessage()` generates concise error descriptions for ~25 different error types (covering git, network, manifest, marketplace, MCP/LSP server, and plugin caching issues), while `getErrorGuidance()` returns actionable troubleshooting steps or configuration hints for each error type. Together, these enable the UI to display both what went wrong and how users can fix it.

files.name: commands/plugin/PluginOptionsDialog.tsx
files.summary: This file implements an interactive terminal dialog for collecting and validating plugin configuration options. It manages a multi-field form where users step through each setting one at a time, handling text input, keyboard navigation (Tab to advance, Enter to save or submit), and special concerns like masking sensitive fields (passwords) during input and preventing accidental overwrites of saved secrets when reconfiguring. The `buildFinalValues` helper function performs type coercion and filtering to prepare the final payload for saving.

files.name: commands/plugin/PluginOptionsFlow.tsx
files.summary: This file orchestrates a multi-step configuration flow for plugins after installation or enablement. It walks the user through a series of dialogs to fill in unconfigured plugin options—both at the plugin level and per-channel for MCP servers—saving each step via the appropriate storage function. If there are no options to configure, it exits immediately; otherwise, it sequences through each dialog and reports completion or errors back to the caller.

files.name: commands/plugin/PluginTrustWarning.tsx
files.summary: This component renders a warning message to users about plugin trust and security risks. It displays a prominent notice that Anthropic cannot verify or control the contents of plugins, and may append additional context-specific messaging retrieved from marketplace helpers. The warning serves as a critical safety disclaimer before users install or interact with plugins.

files.name: commands/plugin/UnifiedInstalledCell.tsx
files.summary: This React component renders a single list item cell for displaying installed plugins or MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers in a terminal UI. It determines the visual appearance and status information based on the item type (regular plugin, flagged plugin, failed plugin, or MCP server) and its current state (enabled, disabled, connecting, etc.), applying appropriate icons, colors, and status text from a theme system. The component handles selection highlighting and displays metadata like marketplace source alongside status indicators.

files.name: commands/plugin/ValidatePlugin.tsx
files.summary: This React component validates Claude plugin manifest files or directories. It accepts a file path as a prop, runs validation through the `validateManifest` utility, and displays a formatted report showing any errors or warnings found, along with an appropriate success or failure message. The component sets the process exit code based on validation results (0 for success, 1 for validation failure, 2 for unexpected errors) and displays "Running validation..." while the async validation completes.

files.name: commands/plugin/index.tsx
files.summary: This file defines and exports the command metadata for the plugin management feature. It registers a CLI command named "plugin" (with aliases "plugins" and "marketplace") that allows users to manage Claude Code plugins, using lazy loading to defer importing the actual implementation logic until the command is invoked.

files.name: commands/plugin/parseArgs.ts
files.summary: This file parses user input strings into structured plugin subcommand objects, enabling the plugin system to route different operations like install, uninstall, enable, disable, and marketplace management. The parser handles various command aliases and argument formats (like `plugin@marketplace` syntax) to provide a flexible CLI interface for plugin management.

files.name: commands/plugin/plugin.tsx
files.summary: This file is the entry point for a plugin management command. It exports a `call` function that instantiates and renders the `PluginSettings` component, passing along the completion callback and any command arguments. It appears to serve as a bridge between the command framework and the plugin settings UI.

files.name: commands/plugin/pluginDetailsHelpers.tsx
files.summary: This file provides shared utility functions and types for plugin marketplace details screens across the application. It exports type definitions for installable plugins and menu options, helper functions to extract GitHub repository information and build contextual installation menus with user/project/local scope options, and a keyboard hint component that displays available shortcuts for plugin selection actions.

files.name: commands/plugin/usePagination.ts
files.summary: This hook manages pagination and visibility logic for large lists, automatically scrolling to keep the currently selected item in view. It bridges traditional page-based pagination APIs with continuous scrolling behavior, providing utilities to slice data, convert between visible and actual indices, and track scroll position for UI display.

files.name: commands/pr_comments/index.ts
files.summary: This file defines a CLI command that fetches and displays comments from a GitHub pull request, including both PR-level discussions and inline code review comments. It uses the GitHub CLI and API to retrieve comments, formats them with context (file paths, line numbers, and code diffs), and presents them in a readable way to the user.

files.name: commands/privacy-settings/index.ts
files.summary: This file defines a command that allows users to view and update their privacy settings. The command is only enabled for consumer subscribers and dynamically loads its implementation from a separate module when needed.

files.name: commands/privacy-settings/privacy-settings.tsx
files.summary: This file implements a command that displays privacy settings dialogs for Claude's data collection feature (Grove). It first checks if the user qualifies for Grove, then shows either a terms-acceptance dialog for new users or a full privacy settings dialog for existing users who have already made a choice. The command also tracks analytics when users toggle their privacy preference and falls back to a link to the web settings page if the API is unavailable or the user is ineligible.

files.name: commands/rate-limit-options/index.ts
files.summary: Registers a command that displays options to users when they hit a rate limit, but only enables it for Claude AI subscribers. The command is hidden from help documentation and loaded dynamically, suggesting it's triggered internally by the rate-limiting system rather than invoked directly by users.

files.name: commands/rate-limit-options/rate-limit-options.tsx
files.summary: This component displays a dialog menu that presents users with options when they hit Claude's rate limits. Depending on their subscription tier, billing status, and account configuration, it conditionally shows options to upgrade their plan, switch to extra usage (with funds), request additional resources from an admin, or wait for the limit to reset. It handles the selection logic by invoking the appropriate sub-command (upgrade or extra-usage flow) and logs analytics events for each user action.

files.name: commands/release-notes/index.ts
files.summary: This file defines a CLI command that allows users to view release notes. It registers a local, non-interactive command with the command framework and defers the actual implementation to a separate module that gets loaded on demand.

files.name: commands/release-notes/release-notes.ts
files.summary: This command retrieves and displays release notes for the project, attempting to fetch the latest changelog from a remote source with a 500ms timeout before falling back to cached data. If no notes are available either way, it returns a link to the full changelog. The formatted output presents release notes organized by version with bullet points.

files.name: commands/reload-plugins/index.ts
files.summary: Defines a command that refreshes plugin changes in the active session without restarting. This is a Layer-3 operation that applies pending plugin updates and returns structured data (commands, agents, plugins, MCP servers) for UI updates; the actual implementation is lazy-loaded on demand.

files.name: commands/reload-plugins/reload-plugins.ts
files.summary: This command reloads and refreshes all active plugins in the system, then reports back how many plugins, skills, agents, hooks, and servers were successfully loaded. It optionally re-downloads user settings in remote mode to ensure the latest plugin configuration takes effect, and alerts the user to any errors that occurred during the load process.

files.name: commands/remote-env/index.ts
files.summary: This file exports a command definition that allows Claude AI subscribers to configure the default remote environment for teleport sessions. The command is only enabled and visible when the user has an active subscription and the `allow_remote_sessions` policy is permitted, ensuring access control over remote session functionality.

files.name: commands/remote-env/remote-env.tsx
files.summary: This file is a command entry point that instantiates and returns the RemoteEnvironmentDialog component. It serves as the callable interface for a "remote environment" command, accepting a completion callback and rendering the dialog as a React node for display.

files.name: commands/remote-setup/api.ts
files.summary: This file provides API functions for remote setup operations in a Claude development environment. It handles GitHub token import (with careful redaction for security), checks user authentication status, and can create a default cloud environment for new users. The GitHub token import validates credentials against GitHub's API and stores them encrypted on the backend so clone/push operations work immediately without additional OAuth flow.

files.name: commands/remote-setup/index.ts
files.summary: This file defines a command that enables users to set up Claude Code on the web by connecting their GitHub account. It gates the feature behind a feature flag (`tengu_cobalt_lantern`) and a policy check (`allow_remote_sessions`), ensuring the command is only available and visible to users who have both the feature enabled and the appropriate permissions.

files.name: commands/remote-setup/remote-setup.tsx
files.summary: This file implements a CLI command that connects Claude on the web to GitHub by prompting the user to authorize their local GitHub credentials. It checks whether the user is signed into Claude and has a valid GitHub CLI authentication, then guides them through confirming the token transfer to the web app, handling various failure states (missing GitHub CLI, invalid tokens, network errors, etc.) along the way.

files.name: commands/rename/generateSessionName.ts
files.summary: This function generates a short kebab-case name for a conversation session by sending the conversation history to Claude Haiku and parsing its JSON response. It's used to automatically create human-readable identifiers for sessions, with graceful degradation that returns null if the API call fails or produces invalid output.

files.name: commands/rename/index.ts
files.summary: Defines a command that allows users to rename the current conversation, registering it as a local JSX command that executes immediately with an optional name argument and loads its implementation from a separate module.

files.name: commands/rename/rename.ts
files.summary: This command handles renaming a session, either to a user-provided name or by auto-generating one from conversation context. It persists the new name locally and syncs it to the bridge service (claude.ai/code) if available, while preventing teammates in a swarm from renaming their sessions since those names are controlled by the team leader.

files.name: commands/reset-limits/index.js
files.summary: This file provides stub implementations for reset-limits functionality that is disabled by default. It exports placeholder functions that do nothing, allowing the codebase to reference reset-limits commands without requiring the actual feature to be implemented or available.

files.name: commands/resume/index.ts
files.summary: Defines a command that allows users to resume or continue a previous conversation by providing a conversation ID or search term. The command is lazily loaded as a JSX component and registered with aliases to make it easily discoverable in the CLI interface.

files.name: commands/resume/resume.tsx
files.summary:
# Resume Command

This file implements the `/resume` command, which allows users to resume previous conversations. It supports three modes: picking from a list of recent conversations, resuming by session ID, or resuming by custom conversation title. The command handles cross-project scenarios by either resuming directly (same repo worktree) or displaying a command to run in the target directory when conversations exist outside the current project.

files.name: commands/review.ts
files.summary: This file defines two code review commands for a CLI tool: a local `review` command that analyzes pull requests using GitHub CLI and Claude's analysis, and an `ultrareview` command that delegates to a remote service (Claude Code on the web) for more comprehensive bug-finding. The ultrareview command is gated behind a feature flag and displays legal terms/permissions to users before enabling deeper analysis.

files.name: commands/review/UltrareviewOverageDialog.tsx
files.summary: This component renders a confirmation dialog that appears when an organization has exhausted its free Ultrareview quota and is about to incur pay-per-use billing charges. It offers the user two options: proceed with the billable review or cancel, while managing the abort signal to allow graceful cancellation of in-flight operations if the user presses Escape or selects cancel during launch.

files.name: commands/review/reviewRemote.ts
files.summary: This file implements the remote code review launch flow for the `/ultrareview` command. It handles billing eligibility checks (quota, overage permissions, and subscription tier), then initiates a cloud-based review session by either referencing a GitHub PR or bundling the local working tree, registering a background task to stream results back to the user. The system mirrors the existing `/ultraplan` remote execution pattern and defers to server-side billing validation when local checks are inconclusive.

files.name: commands/review/ultrareviewCommand.tsx
files.summary: This file implements the entry point for Claude's "ultrareview" code review command, which performs remote analysis of code repositories. It enforces billing controls by checking usage limits and balance before launching the review, showing user-facing dialogs when free tier limits are exceeded or account balance is insufficient, and only proceeds with the remote review session if the user has available quota or confirms they understand the extra usage charges.

files.name: commands/review/ultrareviewEnabled.ts
files.summary: This file provides a feature gate for the `/ultrareview` command by checking a GrowthBook configuration flag. When disabled, the command is filtered out entirely from the available commands list, ensuring ungated users never see it as an option.

files.name: commands/rewind/index.ts
files.summary: This file defines the metadata and configuration for a "rewind" command that allows users to restore code and conversation history to an earlier state. It registers the command with aliases, type information, and a lazy-loaded implementation, serving as the command's entry point in the CLI system.

files.name: commands/rewind/rewind.ts
files.summary: This command opens a message selector UI to let users choose a previous message in the conversation to rewind to, then returns a skip result to prevent appending additional messages to the chat history.

files.name: commands/sandbox-toggle/index.ts
files.summary: This file defines a CLI command that allows users to toggle and configure sandboxing settings for command execution. It displays the current sandbox state (enabled/disabled, auto-allow status, fallback permissions) along with dependency and policy lock information, and only appears on supported platforms where sandboxing is enabled.

files.name: commands/sandbox-toggle/sandbox-toggle.tsx
files.summary: This file implements a command handler for toggling and configuring sandbox settings. It performs platform compatibility checks (validating support for macOS, Linux, and WSL2), validates enterprise policy constraints, and then either displays an interactive settings UI or processes subcommands—specifically the `exclude` subcommand, which allows users to prevent sandboxing from running on specific command patterns. The command acts as an entry point between the CLI and the underlying sandbox management system.

files.name: commands/security-review.ts
files.summary: This file defines a security review command that analyzes pending code changes on a Git branch for vulnerabilities. It embeds a detailed security analysis prompt (with specific vulnerability categories, methodology, and output formatting requirements) and processes it by executing allowed Git commands to gather diff and commit information before passing the complete analysis context to an AI model. The command is exported as a plugin that can be invoked to perform automated security reviews of pull request changes.

files.name: commands/session/index.ts
files.summary: This file defines and exports a CLI command that displays the remote session URL and QR code to users. The command is only enabled and visible when the application is running in remote mode, determined by checking the bootstrap state.

files.name: commands/session/session.tsx
files.summary: This file implements a React component that displays remote session information to users. When the Claude app runs in remote mode (started with `--remote`), it shows a QR code that can be scanned to access the session in a browser, along with the direct URL. The component handles QR code generation asynchronously and allows users to dismiss the display by pressing Escape.

files.name: commands/share/index.js
files.summary: This is a stub implementation of a share command that is permanently disabled and hidden from the user interface. It appears to be a placeholder for a share feature that either hasn't been implemented yet or has been intentionally deactivated.

files.name: commands/skills/index.ts
files.summary: This file exports a command definition that enables users to list available skills in the application. It's a metadata configuration for a locally-rendered JSX command that references the actual skill-listing logic implemented in a separate module.

files.name: commands/skills/skills.tsx
files.summary: This file implements a command handler that renders a skills menu interface. It serves as the entry point for the skills command, receiving a callback and command context, then returning a React component that displays available skills and handles the user's exit action.

files.name: commands/stats/index.ts
files.summary: Defines a command registration that exposes Claude Code usage statistics and activity to users through a local JSX interface, with lazy-loading of the actual implementation.

files.name: commands/stats/stats.tsx
files.summary: This file implements a command entry point that renders the Stats UI component. It serves as a bridge between the command system and the Stats component, receiving a callback (`onDone`) to handle when the user closes the stats interface.

files.name: commands/status/index.ts
files.summary: Defines metadata and lazy-loading configuration for the `status` command, which displays Claude Code's system information including version, model, account details, API connectivity, and tool availability. The command is marked as immediate-execution and loads its implementation from a separate module on demand.

files.name: commands/status/status.tsx
files.summary: This file implements a "Status" command that renders the Settings component with the Status tab pre-selected. It serves as a command entry point that allows users to access application status information through the unified settings interface.

files.name: commands/statusline.tsx
files.summary: This file defines a built-in command that sets up Claude Code's status line UI by delegating to a specialized subagent. When invoked, it creates an agent tool with the "statusline-setup" subagent type, allowing users to configure their editor's status line based on their shell PS1 configuration. The command is restricted to interactive mode and permits only specific tools for reading shell configs and editing Claude settings.

files.name: commands/stickers/index.ts
files.summary: Registers a local CLI command that allows users to order Claude Code stickers, with lazy-loading of the command's implementation. This command requires interactive mode and serves as the entry point definition for the stickers ordering feature.

files.name: commands/stickers/stickers.ts
files.summary: This command opens a Sticker Mule promotional page in the user's browser, returning either a success message or a fallback URL if the browser launch fails. It appears to be a utility command for accessing branded stickers related to the application.

files.name: commands/summary/index.js
files.summary: This is a stub implementation for a summary command that is disabled by default and hidden from user-facing command lists. It appears to serve as a placeholder or template for command infrastructure, allowing the system to register a command entry without providing active functionality.

files.name: commands/tag/index.ts
files.summary: This file defines a command registration entry for a "tag" feature that allows users to toggle searchable tags on the current session. The command is only enabled for users with the "ant" user type and is implemented as a locally-executed JSX command that accepts a tag name as input. It serves as a configuration/export point that lazy-loads the actual command implementation from a separate module.

files.name: commands/tag/tag.tsx
files.summary: This file implements a `/tag` command that allows users to add searchable labels to sessions. When a user runs `/tag <name>`, the command either adds a new tag to the current session or—if that tag already exists—prompts for confirmation to remove it, supporting a toggle workflow. The command sanitizes input, persists tags to storage, and logs analytics events at each step.

files.name: commands/tasks/index.ts
files.summary: Registers a CLI command that lists and manages background tasks, with an alias for backwards compatibility or alternative naming. The command lazily loads its implementation from a separate module to keep the command registry lightweight.

files.name: commands/tasks/tasks.tsx
files.summary: This file is a command entry point that renders a dialog for displaying background tasks to the user. It implements the standard command interface by accepting a context and completion callback, then returns a React component that manages the task display UI.

files.name: commands/teleport/index.js
files.summary: This is a stub implementation of a teleport command that is permanently disabled and hidden from users. It appears to be a placeholder reserving the command name for potential future implementation while preventing it from being active or visible in the current system.

files.name: commands/terminalSetup/index.ts
files.summary: This file defines a command that guides users through terminal-specific keyboard setup, enabling enhanced key bindings like Shift+Enter for newlines. It conditionally hides the command for terminals that natively support the CSI u / Kitty keyboard protocol (Ghostty, Kitty, iTerm2, WezTerm), and customizes its description based on the detected terminal type.

files.name: commands/theme/index.ts
files.summary: This file registers a CLI command that allows users to change the application theme, delegating the actual implementation to a separate theme.js module that loads on demand.

files.name: commands/theme/theme.tsx
files.summary: This file implements a command that displays a theme picker UI, allowing users to select and apply a new theme to the application. When a theme is selected, it updates the global theme state and notifies the command system of the change; if dismissed, it logs the dismissal as a system event.

files.name: commands/thinkback-play/index.ts
files.summary: This file defines a hidden command that triggers the playback of a "thinkback" animation after the thinkback skill completes content generation. The command is feature-gated behind a Statsig flag and only available in interactive mode, serving as an internal animation trigger rather than a user-facing command.

files.name: commands/thinkback-play/thinkback-play.ts
files.summary: This command plays a visual animation from the installed Thinkback plugin by locating the plugin's skill directory and invoking its animation playback function. It serves as the user-facing entry point for the "play" variant of the thinkback feature, with fallback messages if the plugin isn't installed or its path is unavailable.

files.name: commands/thinkback/index.ts
files.summary: Registers a "think-back" command that displays a user's 2025 Claude Code Year in Review feature. The command is gated behind a Statsig feature flag (`tengu_thinkback`) and lazily loads its implementation from a separate module only when enabled.

files.name: commands/upgrade/index.ts
files.summary: Defines a CLI command that prompts users to upgrade their Claude AI subscription for higher rate limits and additional Opus access. The command is only available for non-enterprise users and can be globally disabled via an environment variable.

files.name: commands/upgrade/upgrade.tsx
files.summary: The upgrade command handles user requests to upgrade their Claude AI subscription plan. It first checks if the user is already on the maximum subscription tier (Claude Max 20x) and directs them to use `/login` for API billing if so; otherwise, it opens the Claude AI upgrade page in a browser and initiates a login flow to authenticate the upgrade.

files.name: commands/usage/index.ts
files.summary: Registers a "usage" command that displays plan usage limits for Claude AI, delegating the actual implementation to a separate module that loads on demand.

files.name: commands/usage/usage.tsx
files.summary: This file implements a command handler that displays the Usage tab of the application's Settings panel. When invoked, it renders the Settings component pre-configured to show usage information and handles cleanup through the provided callback. It serves as the entry point for a "usage" command in what appears to be a command-driven UI system.

files.name: commands/version.ts
files.summary: This file implements a `version` command that displays the current application version and optionally its build timestamp. It's only enabled for a specific user type (`ant`) and appears designed to help users verify which version of the application they're running, distinct from any auto-updated version available.

files.name: commands/vim/index.ts
files.summary: Defines the metadata and loading configuration for a Vim mode toggle command that allows users to switch between Vim and Normal editing modes in an interactive environment. This serves as the command's entry point, declaring its properties and lazy-loading the actual implementation from a separate module.

files.name: commands/vim/vim.ts
files.summary: This command toggles the editor mode between vim and normal (readline) bindings in the application's global configuration. It handles backward compatibility by treating legacy 'emacs' mode as 'normal', logs the mode change for analytics, and returns a user-friendly message explaining the current keybinding scheme.

files.name: commands/voice/index.ts
files.summary: This file exports a command definition that enables users to toggle voice mode functionality. It checks feature flags to determine whether voice mode is available and visible, and lazily loads the actual voice command implementation only when needed.

files.name: commands/voice/voice.ts
files.summary: This file implements the `/voice` command, which toggles voice mode on and off. When enabling voice mode, it performs a series of preflight checks—verifying authentication, microphone availability, recording tool dependencies, and OS-level microphone permissions—and provides specific guidance if any check fails. Once enabled, it displays the keybinding for push-to-talk activation and optionally hints about the dictation language being used.

files.name: components/AgentProgressLine.tsx
files.summary: This component renders a single line of progress information for an agent execution in a terminal UI. It displays the agent type/name, tool usage count, token consumption, and status messages (like "Initializing," "Running in the background," or "Done") with tree-structure characters to show hierarchy. The component adapts its display based on whether the agent is async, resolved, or still running, and conditionally shows details to avoid clutter when tasks run in the background.

files.name: components/App.tsx
files.summary: App is the root provider component that wraps the entire application with three nested context providers: FpsMetricsProvider (for performance tracking), StatsProvider (for statistics management), and AppStateProvider (for application state management). It accepts initial state and children, and serves as the single entry point to establish all global context needed for interactive sessions.

files.name: components/ApproveApiKey.tsx
files.summary: This component renders a dialog that prompts users to approve or reject a custom API key detected in their environment. When the user selects "yes," the key is added to an approved list in global config and the callback fires with `true`; selecting "no" (the recommended option) rejects the key, adds it to a rejected list, and fires the callback with `false`. It serves as a security checkpoint to control which API keys the application will use.

files.name: components/AutoModeOptInDialog.tsx
files.summary: This component renders a dialog that prompts users to opt into Claude's auto mode, which allows Claude to handle permission checks automatically. It offers three options: enable auto mode, enable it as the default mode, or decline. The component logs analytics events for each user choice and persists the selection to user settings via the `updateSettingsForSource` utility.

files.name: components/AutoUpdater.tsx
files.summary: This component automatically checks for and installs updates to the Claude CLI application at regular intervals (every 30 minutes). It detects the current installation type (global npm, local npm, or development), fetches the latest available version from a configured channel, respects server-side version caps, and executes the appropriate installation method. It also tracks update success/failure metrics and displays status messages to the user about installation progress and any errors encountered.

files.name: components/AutoUpdaterWrapper.tsx
files.summary: AutoUpdaterWrapper is a routing component that detects the system's installation type (native vs. package manager) on mount and conditionally renders the appropriate auto-updater implementation. It defers rendering until the installation type is determined, then delegates to either NativeAutoUpdater or PackageManagerAutoUpdater based on the detection result, with an optimization to skip detection entirely if auto-updates are disabled.

files.name: components/AwsAuthStatusBox.tsx
files.summary: This component displays a status box for AWS authentication, showing authentication progress, output logs, and any errors that occur during the authentication process. It subscribes to a centralized `AwsAuthStatusManager` to receive real-time status updates and conditionally renders a bordered box containing the authentication title, the last five lines of output (with URLs automatically converted to clickable links), and error messages. The box only appears when authentication is in progress or when an error has occurred.

files.name: components/BaseTextInput.tsx
files.summary:
# BaseTextInput

This component renders a text input field for terminal-based UIs, managing cursor positioning, text highlighting, placeholder display, and paste event handling. It serves as the foundational component for various prompt input types, coordinating multiple hooks to handle cursor declaration, paste detection, and placeholder rendering while supporting optional syntax highlighting and argument hints.

files.name: components/BashModeProgress.tsx
files.summary: This component displays the real-time progress of a bash command execution. It renders the user's bash input at the top and conditionally shows either a detailed shell progress message with output and timing information, or falls back to a generic tool progress renderer when progress data is unavailable. The component is optimized with React Compiler for performance tracking of its inputs.

files.name: components/BridgeDialog.tsx
files.summary: This component renders a terminal UI dialog for managing a remote control bridge connection. It displays the bridge's current connection status (connected/disconnected/error), generates QR codes for session URLs, and provides keyboard controls to disconnect (d key) or toggle the QR code display (spacebar). The dialog also shows context information like the repository name and git branch, plus optional verbose debugging details, matching the format of a standalone bridge application.

files.name: components/BypassPermissionsModeDialog.tsx
files.summary: This component displays a warning dialog that asks users to confirm they want to enable "Bypass Permissions mode," which allows Claude Code to execute potentially dangerous commands without requesting approval. If the user accepts, it logs the action, updates settings to skip future permission prompts, and proceeds; if they decline or close the dialog, the application shuts down gracefully. The component emphasizes security risks and requires explicit user consent before enabling this high-risk mode.

files.name: components/ChannelDowngradeDialog.tsx
files.summary: This component renders a dialog that appears when users attempt to switch from the "latest" release channel to the "stable" channel. It presents the user with three options: allow a potential downgrade to the stable version, stay on their current version until stable catches up, or cancel the operation. The component handles the user's selection and communicates their choice back via a callback.

files.name: components/ClaudeCodeHint/PluginHintMenu.tsx
files.summary: This component renders a dialog that prompts users to install a recommended plugin when a command suggests one. It displays the plugin name, marketplace, and optional description, then offers three choices: install it, decline, or disable future hints. The dialog automatically dismisses with a "no" response after 30 seconds if the user doesn't interact with it.

files.name: components/ClaudeInChromeOnboarding.tsx
files.summary: This component displays an onboarding dialog for the "Claude in Chrome" beta feature. It informs users about the Chrome extension's capabilities (browser control, form filling, screenshots, GIFs, debugging), checks if the extension is installed, and shows conditional messaging about permissions based on installation status. The dialog logs an analytics event when shown, marks the onboarding as complete in global config, and allows users to dismiss it by pressing Enter or clicking cancel.

files.name: components/ClaudeMdExternalIncludesDialog.tsx
files.summary: This component renders a dialog warning users when a project's CLAUDE.md file imports files from outside the working directory, and prompts them to approve or deny external imports. It logs user decisions and updates project configuration accordingly to track whether external imports have been approved and whether the warning has been shown.

files.name: components/ClickableImageRef.tsx
files.summary: Renders an image reference (e.g., "[Image #1]") as a clickable terminal link that opens the stored image file in the default viewer. If the terminal doesn't support hyperlinks or the image file isn't found, it falls back to displaying styled text instead. The component supports custom background colors and a selected state for UI styling.

files.name: components/CompactSummary.tsx
files.summary: This component renders a display summary of a user message, with two different views depending on whether summarization metadata exists. When metadata is present, it shows details about a "summarized conversation" (including how many messages were summarized and the direction), and switches between showing metadata details in normal mode or the full message text in transcript mode. Without metadata, it displays a default "compact summary" label with an option to expand the full history. The component primarily exists to provide a compact UI representation of messages in a REPL-like conversation interface, with keyboard shortcuts to toggle between collapsed and expanded transcript views.

files.name: components/ConfigurableShortcutHint.tsx
files.summary: This component wraps keyboard shortcut hints to display user-configured keybindings with fallback support. It queries the keybinding system for the actual shortcut mapped to a specific action and context, falling back to a provided default if the configuration isn't available, then renders the shortcut with optional styling like bold text or parentheses wrapping.

files.name: components/ContextSuggestions.tsx
files.summary: This component renders a list of context suggestions in a terminal UI, displaying each suggestion with its severity level, title, optional token savings, and detailed description. It returns `null` if no suggestions are provided, making it a conditionally-rendered informational panel for the CLI tool.

files.name: components/CoordinatorAgentStatus.tsx
files.summary:
# CoordinatorAgentStatus

This component renders a steerable panel of background agent tasks that appears below the prompt input. It displays running or retained local agent tasks with their status (elapsed time, token counts, queue depth), allows users to enter a task to view/control it, and automatically evicts tasks past their expiration deadline. The panel updates every second to keep elapsed timers current and responds to user interactions like selection and dismissal.

files.name: components/CostThresholdDialog.tsx
files.summary: This component renders a modal dialog that alerts users when they've reached a $5 spending threshold on the Anthropic API during their session. It displays the spending amount, provides a link to documentation about cost monitoring, and includes a confirmation button to dismiss the alert.

files.name: components/CtrlOToExpand.tsx
files.summary: This file provides a React component and utility for displaying a "(ctrl+o to expand)" hint to users. It includes a `SubAgentContext` to suppress duplicate hints in nested agent outputs, a `CtrlOToExpand` component that renders the hint visually (when not in a sub-agent or virtual list), and a string utility function for terminal output. The hint tells users they can press Ctrl+O to expand and view the full transcript.

files.name: components/CustomSelect/SelectMulti.tsx
files.summary: This component renders a multi-select dropdown interface for terminal/CLI applications built with Ink. It manages selection state, displays a scrollable list of options (both regular and input-editable variants), handles keyboard navigation, and optionally shows a submit button. The component supports features like custom styling, image pasting, external editor integration, and callbacks for when users navigate, select items, or submit their choices.

files.name: components/CustomSelect/index.ts
files.summary: This file serves as the public API gateway for the CustomSelect component library, re-exporting selection utilities and types from its submodules to provide a unified entry point for consumers. It exposes both the multi-select functionality and single-select components along with their type definitions.

files.name: components/CustomSelect/option-map.ts
files.summary: This file defines a specialized Map structure for managing select dropdown options with bidirectional navigation support. It transforms a flat array of options into a linked-list-like structure where each option tracks its previous and next neighbors, enabling efficient keyboard navigation and traversal in custom select components.

files.name: components/CustomSelect/select-option.tsx
files.summary: This component wraps a `ListItem` to render a single selectable option within a custom select dropdown, passing through state like focus and selection status along with optional scroll indicators and descriptions. It serves as a lightweight presentational layer that adapts the select-specific props to the generic `ListItem` component's interface.

Their work touches core systems like the query engine, task management, tools framework, and notably extensive work on the bridge API layer for remote control sessions. However, with only 2 commits, their contribution appears to be primarily architectural or organizational in nature rather than ongoing feature development.
